title: Backup completeness — unprotected workloads with regulated data
exclusions: Does NOT assess backup frequency; see UC-22.45.1 for RPO.
evidence: Daily 'backup_coverage_gap' writes unprotected-workload findings to compliance_summary.

compliance.assurance_rationale: CP-9 requires backup of system-level and user-level information — uncovered workloads fail the control.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 CP-9 (System backup) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.3: Backup completeness — unprotected workloads with regulated data.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.12 requires comprehensive backup; a gap is a direct finding.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that DORA Art.12 (Backup policies and recovery methods) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.3: Backup completeness — unprotected workloads with regulated data.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: ITGC backup and restore — coverage is the threshold question.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOX ITGC ITGC.Operations.Backup (Backup and restore) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.45.3: Backup completeness — unprotected workloads with regulated data.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.45.3' running on catalogue-defined data sources (see UC detailedImplementation), archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: When a workload tagged regulated=true has no successful backup within the configured window (default 24h for tier-1), the UC fires naming the workload, tier, last_success.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Workloads with a successful backup within the window do NOT fire.

spl:
| rest /services/backup/inventory splunk_server=* | where regulated="true"
| stats max(last_success) as last_ok by workload tier
| lookup dr_tiers.csv tier OUTPUT expected_interval_hours
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_ok)/3600,0)
| where hours_since>expected_interval_hours
| table tier workload last_ok hours_since expected_interval_hours
description: Cross-checks the backup inventory against the regulated-workload asset list. Any covered workload without a successful backup in its window becomes a finding.
value: Coverage gaps are the single most common backup finding at audit; continuous evidence eliminates surprise findings.
implementation: (1) Tag regulated workloads; (2) publish dr_tiers.csv; (3) run daily; (4) escalate via SOAR.
visualization: Heatmap of workload × day, bar chart of gaps by tier, single-value '% of regulated workloads protected'.
knownFalsePositives: Backup test failures during quarterly DR drills, vendor-driven restoration tests, or planned platform migrations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: See subcategory preamble..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Tag regulated workloads; (2) publish dr_tiers.csv; (3) run daily; (4) escalate via SOAR.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/backup/inventory splunk_server=* | where regulated="true"
| stats max(last_success) as last_ok by workload tier
| lookup dr_tiers.csv tier OUTPUT expected_interval_hours
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_ok)/3600,0)
| where hours_since>expected_interval_hours
| table tier workload last_ok hours_since expected_interval_hours
```

Understanding this SPL

**Backup completeness — unprotected workloads with regulated data** — Coverage gaps are the single most common backup finding at audit; continuous evidence eliminates surprise findings.

Documented **Data sources**: See subcategory preamble. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Filters the current rows with `where regulated="true"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **workload tier**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_since** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where hours_since>expected_interval_hours` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: expected_interval_hours, hours_since, last_ok, tier, workload.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with backup, restore, or test evidence systems and compare with the GRC system so recovery proof matches the control narrative.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Heatmap of workload × day, bar chart of gaps by tier, single-value '% of regulated workloads protected'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show backups were tested and restorable when regulators or disaster recovery need proof.
